message:
feat(server,search-core,conformance): add TUI keymap, screen module updates, and resource description parity test

- server/tui_keymap.rs: keyboard shortcut mapping updates for TUI
  navigation and command dispatch
- server/tui_screens/mod.rs: screen module declaration updates for
  new visualization screens
- search-core/fs_bridge.rs: filesystem bridge refinements from
  concurrent development
- conformance/tests/resource_description_parity.rs: new parity test
  validating that MCP resource descriptions match the Python reference
  implementation
- .beads/issues.jsonl: sync issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2tnl.5","title":"[track] Hybrid Fusion, Two-Tier Refinement, and Reranking","description":"## Purpose
Implement hybrid search quality stack: lexical + semantic candidate fusion, progressive two-tier refinement, and reranking.

## Scope
- Candidate generation orchestration.
- RRF fusion with deterministic tie-breakers.
- Two-tier fast→quality refinement path.
- Cross-encoder reranking and score blending.
- Explainable score decomposition and cache/warmup behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Candidate orchestration + RRF fusion + two-tier refinement are implemented.
- Optional reranker integration works with fallback behavior.
- Hybrid responses include merged explainability metadata and cache/warmup controls.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"track","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.533469337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:16:38.682509636Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hybrid","quality","reranking","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.533469337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:35.240065196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:36.432717942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:36.633790517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":233,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 5 reasoning:

- Hybrid quality gains come from principled fusion (RRF) and progressive refinement, not from ad-hoc score mixing.
- Two-tier search is specifically chosen to protect latency while still allowing high-quality refinement.
- Reranker is optional-at-runtime but mandatory-in-design for top-end precision.
- Explainability is required so operators can trust why a result ranked highly, especially when multiple scoring stages are combined.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:39:13Z"}]}

- mode-aware candidate multipliers.
- deterministic dedupe and merge preparation.

Hybrid candidate selection stage used by fusion/rerank pipeline.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Candidate orchestration merges lexical and semantic pools with clear stage boundaries.
2. Candidate budgets and quotas are configurable per mode and query class.

- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","notes":"Progress: converted candidate budget arithmetic in hybrid_candidates to deterministic integer-scaled math (no float->usize casts), kept mode-aware/query-class-aware derivation, and revalidated module tests + db search-v3 check. Coordination updates posted in Agent Mail thread br-2tnl.5.1.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:04.125917254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:16:38.067327253Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["candidates","hybrid","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:46.090797759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:46.304537776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:04.125917254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.10","title":"Two-Tier Search Hardening: Thread-Safe Init, Embedder Fallbacks, Progressive Refinement","description":"## Background

Code review of the two-tier search implementation identified three areas for hardening:

1. **Lazy initialization race condition** - `try_two_tier_search` may create duplicate `TwoTierBridge` instances when called concurrently before initialization completes.

2. **Zero-filled quality embeddings** - When quality embedder is unavailable, documents get zero-filled quality embeddings which provide no meaningful similarity signal.

3. **Progressive refinement not implemented** - The current `TwoTierBridge::search()` only uses fast embeddings; the full fast→quality progressive refinement workflow is stubbed.

## Goals

- Ensure thread-safe single initialization of embedder bridges
- Provide meaningful fallback behavior when quality embedder is unavailable
- Implement full progressive search with streaming/async quality refinement
- Maintain sub-millisecond fast-path latency
- Add comprehensive observability for debugging and monitoring

## Architecture Context

The two-tier system uses:
- **Fast tier**: potion-128M (Model2Vec, ~0ms inference, 256 dims)
- **Quality tier**: MiniLM-L6-v2 (FastEmbed/ONNX, ~128ms, 384 dims)

Both embedders use global `OnceLock` singletons for auto-initialization.

## Files of Interest

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/two_tier.rs` - Core index and search
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/auto_init.rs` - Global context
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/model2vec.rs` - Fast embedder
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/fastembed.rs` - Quality embedder
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs` - TwoTierBridge integration

## Success Criteria

- Zero duplicate bridge creation under concurrent access
- Quality-unavailable documents still participate in semantic search meaningfully
- Progressive refinement yields improved relevance with transparent latency
- All tests pass with -D warnings clippy","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:27.353873587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:35:04.614473056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hardening","search","semantic","two-tier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:27.353873587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":514,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started work on child br-2tnl.5.10.2 (forced in_progress due blocked parent-child constraints). Implementing quality-embedding fallback hardening and keeping validations attached to that child.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:07:14Z"},{"id":523,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"OVERALL SUCCESS CRITERIA CHECKLIST (aggregate of child beads):

## Core Implementation (P0)
- [ ] T5.10.1: Race condition fixed - only one bridge created under concurrent access
- [ ] T5.10.2: Quality fallback - has_quality flag implemented, no zero-vectors stored
- [ ] T5.10.3: Progressive refinement - search yields Initial then Refined phases

## Testing (P1)
- [ ] T5.10.4: 7+ unit tests for thread-safe init (including 100-thread stress test)
- [ ] T5.10.5: 8+ unit tests for quality fallback (including boundary tests)
- [ ] T5.10.6: 12+ unit tests for progressive refinement (including timeout, edge cases)
- [ ] T5.10.7: E2E script passes all 7 test functions
- [ ] T5.10.10: Benchmarks pass performance targets (<5ms fast search for 10K docs)

## Observability (P1)
- [ ] T5.10.8: All 4 metrics structs implemented
- [ ] T5.10.8: All 8 tracing spans emitted
- [ ] T5.10.8: User-friendly messaging for degraded mode

## Optimization (P2)
- [ ] T5.10.9: Baseline established before optimization
- [ ] T5.10.9: At least one optimization phase completed with measurable improvement

## User Experience
- [ ] Search results include was_refined and explain fields
- [ ] Health check reports two-tier status with install hints if degraded
- [ ] TUI/CLI shows quality coverage stats
- [ ] Configuration via env vars (TWO_TIER_FAST_ONLY, TWO_TIER_QUALITY_TIMEOUT_MS, etc.)

## Definition of Done
All P0 and P1 items complete. P2 items can be deferred to follow-up.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:30:05Z"},{"id":524,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRITICAL GAPS IDENTIFIED FROM CODE REVIEW:

1. **INDEX PERSISTENCE**: Current implementation is in-memory only. On restart, all embeddings are lost and must be recomputed. Consider:
   - Persist index to disk (memmap or sqlite blob)
   - Or accept recompute-on-restart for MVP

2. **INDEX SIZE LIMITS**: No bounds on index growth. For large projects:
   - Add configurable MAX_INDEX_DOCS (default: 100K)
   - Implement LRU eviction for oldest/least-accessed docs
   - Add metric for index memory usage

3. **DOCUMENT UPDATES/REMOVAL**: No API to update or remove docs from index:
   - add_document() always appends
   - Need remove_document(doc_id) for deleted messages
   - Need update_document() or remove+add

4. **LARGE DOCUMENT HANDLING**: Embedding very long texts:
   - Model2Vec has implicit token limit (~512 tokens)
   - FastEmbed truncates at 256 tokens by default
   - Add MAX_EMBED_TEXT_LENGTH config (default: 4096 chars)
   - Truncate or chunk long documents

5. **EMBEDDER HEALTH DURING SESSION**: Code at line 806 checks is_available() but:
   - What if embedder crashes mid-session?
   - Add periodic health check and recovery
   - Log warning if embedder becomes unavailable

6. **LEGACY FALLBACK TESTING**: Line 815 falls back to SemanticBridge:
   - Ensure T5.10.7 E2E tests this fallback path
   - Test: disable two-tier, verify legacy semantic still works","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:32:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-3vwi.9.3","title":"Create soak/stress replay harness for multi-project heavy workloads","description":"Replay large synthetic and captured workloads across multiple projects to validate stability, graceful degradation, and long-session reliability under realistic operator activity patterns.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

All acceptance criteria met: deterministic seeding, JSON artifacts, threshold rules, one-command CI execution.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf","reliability","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3vwi.9.3","depends_on_id":"br-3vwi.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:49:17.332651459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3vwi.9.3","depends_on_id":"br-3vwi.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:49:18.125853314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-3w7uy","title":"Track 4: Monitoring & Analytics — robot reservations, metrics, health, analytics","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:27.456011242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:24:28.089501540Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:24:28.089482835Z","close_reason":"Track 4 complete: all children (reservations, metrics, health, analytics, unit tests) implemented and tested","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3w7uy","depends_on_id":"br-58bpv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:21:19.155835212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":290,"issue_id":"br-3w7uy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Track 4: Monitoring & Analytics Commands

## T4.1: `am robot reservations`

File reservation view with TTL warnings and conflict detection.
Agents need to know: what am I blocking? What's about to expire?
What conflicts exist?

Output:
```
my_reservations[3]{path,exclusive,remaining,granted_at}:
  src/auth/**,true,40m,2h ago
  tests/**,false,53m,1h ago
  docs/API.md,true,3m ⚠,45m ago

all_active[5]{agent,path,exclusive,remaining}:
  BlueLake,src/auth/**,true,40m
  RedFox,src/middleware/**,true,2h
  GreenCastle,src/db/**,true,1h
  BlueLake,tests/**,false,53m
  BlueLake,docs/API.md,true,3m ⚠

conflicts[0]:

expiring_soon[1]{agent,path,remaining}:
  BlueLake,docs/API.md,3m

_actions[1]: am file_reservations renew P BlueLake --paths docs/API.md --extend 3600
```

Flags:
  --agent A      Filter to specific agent (default: auto-detect)
  --all          Show all agents' reservations
  --conflicts    Only show conflicting reservations
  --expiring N   Show reservations expiring within N minutes (default: 10)

## T4.2: `am robot metrics`

Tool performance summary from tooling/metrics resource.
Shows agents which tools are slow, erroring, or heavily used.

Output:
```
summary:
  total_calls: 1234
  total_errors: 12
  error_rate: 0.97%
  avg_latency_ms: 45

tools[10]{name,calls,errors,error_pct,avg_ms,p95_ms,p99_ms}:
  fetch_inbox,450,0,0.0,12,25,45
  send_message,200,3,1.5,89,150,200
  search_messages,180,1,0.6,120,250,400
  file_reservation_paths,150,0,0.0,8,15,20
  acknowledge_message,100,0,0.0,5,10,15
  register_agent,50,2,4.0,30,50,80
  health_check,40,0,0.0,3,5,8
  summarize_thread,30,5,16.7,2500,4000,5000
  macro_start_session,20,1,5.0,150,300,500
  whois,14,0,0.0,6,10,15

_alerts[1]{severity,summary}:
  warn,summarize_thread has 16.7% error rate (5/30 calls)
```

Includes connection probes, diagnostics, and remediation hints.


## T4.4: `am robot analytics`

Anomaly insights from the Analytics TUI screen.
Presents anomaly cards in agent-friendly format with actionable remediation.

Output:
```
anomalies[2]:
  - severity: warn
    confidence: 0.85
    category: ack_sla
    headline: 2 messages pending ack > 30 minutes
    rationale: Messages 142, 156 have ack_required=true but no ack_ts
    remediation: am mail ack --project P --agent A 142 156
    related_screen: inbox

  - severity: info
    confidence: 0.72
    category: reservation_ttl
    headline: 1 reservation expiring in < 5 minutes
    rationale: docs/API.md exclusive lock expires in 3 minutes
    remediation: am file_reservations renew P A --paths docs/API.md --extend 3600
    related_screen: reservations
```

 {"id":"br-43g80","title":"T15.2: Performance benchmarks for rendering budget enforcement","description":"Create benchmarks that verify frame render time stays under 16ms budget.

BENCHMARKS:
- Dashboard render with 10K events in buffer and all charts active
- Messages screen with 1000 messages and markdown preview
- Timeline with 10K events and LogViewer
- Canvas Braille heatmap with full data
- Ambient effect rendering (plasma, metaballs, doom fire)
- Theme switch timing (must be < 1 frame)
- Tree widget with 500-node thread

Use criterion for statistical benchmarks. Run with `cargo bench -p mcp-agent-mail-server`.

the resulting diagrams on the Contacts and Threads screens.

RENDERING PIPELINE:
1. Generate Mermaid syntax from T4.1

3. Layout with ftui_extras::mermaid::Layout
4. Render to Canvas widget

INTERACTION:
- 'g' keybinding toggles graph view overlay
- Graph renders in a panel that overlays the normal screen content
- Panel has rounded borders, title "Communication Graph" or "Thread Flow"
- Panel is dismissible with Escape or 'g' again

DEBOUNCING:
- Re-generate Mermaid syntax at most once per second
- Cache rendered Canvas output between data changes
- Only re-render when underlying data (contacts, messages) changes

 {"id":"br-56kxi","title":"R4.4: Implement am robot analytics — anomaly insights with severity, confidence, remediation commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:19.365314806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:21:46.496641646Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:21:46.496617350Z","close_reason":"Implemented: robot analytics with 5 anomaly checks (ack_sla, reservation_expiry, stale_agents, high_volume, inbox_backlog), confidence scores, remediation commands","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-56kxi","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:54.588180653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":316,"issue_id":"br-56kxi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R4.4: `am robot analytics`

## What
Anomaly detection and insights. Presents anomaly cards in agent-friendly format with actionable remediation commands. This is the CLI equivalent of the TUI Analytics screen.

## Anomaly Detection Categories

### 1. ack_sla — Acknowledgment SLA violations
- **Trigger**: Messages with ack_required=true, ack_ts IS NULL, created_at < now - 1800s (30min)
- **Severity**: warn (30min-2h), error (>2h)
- **Remediation**: `am mail ack --project P --agent A <message_ids>`

### 2. reservation_ttl — Expiring reservations
- **Trigger**: Reservations with remaining < 5 minutes
- **Severity**: info (<10min), warn (<5min), error (<1min)
- **Remediation**: `am file_reservations renew P A --paths <path> --extend 3600`

### 3. tool_reliability — Tool error rates
- **Trigger**: Tool error_pct > 10%
- **Severity**: warn (10-25%), error (>25%)
- **Remediation**: `am robot metrics` (investigate)

### 4. communication_gap — Silent agents
- **Trigger**: Agent last_active > 2 hours AND has active reservations
- **Severity**: info (2-6h), warn (>6h)
- **Remediation**: `am robot agents --active` (check who's working)

### 5. inbox_overflow — Large unread count
- **Trigger**: Unread messages > 20 for any agent
- **Severity**: warn (>20), error (>50)
- **Remediation**: `am robot inbox --limit 50`

## Output Format
```
anomalies[2]:
  - severity: warn
    confidence: 0.85
    category: ack_sla
    headline: 2 messages pending ack > 30 minutes
    rationale: Messages 142, 156 have ack_required=true but no ack_ts
    remediation: am mail ack --project P --agent A 142 156
    related_screen: inbox

  - severity: info
    confidence: 0.72
    category: reservation_ttl
    headline: 1 reservation expiring in < 5 minutes
    rationale: docs/API.md exclusive lock expires in 3 minutes
    remediation: am file_reservations renew P A --paths docs/API.md --extend 3600
    related_screen: reservations

summary:
  total_anomalies: 2
  by_severity: warn(1),info(1)
  by_category: ack_sla(1),reservation_ttl(1)
```

## Confidence Scoring
- ack_sla: 0.85 base (clear SLA violation)
- reservation_ttl: 0.72 base (agent might intentionally let it expire)
- tool_reliability: 0.60 base (could be transient)
- communication_gap: 0.50 base (agent might be in deep work)
- inbox_overflow: 0.65 base (might be intentional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 5 anomaly categories implemented
- Severity escalation works (warn→error based on thresholds)
- Remediation commands are valid, copy-paste ready
- Confidence scores reflect detection certainty
- Empty anomalies produce clean output
- related_screen field helps agents navigate to relevant data
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:28:25Z"},{"id":341,"issue_id":"br-56kxi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R4.4: `am robot analytics`

## What
Anomaly detection and insights. Presents anomaly cards in agent-friendly format with actionable remediation commands. This is the CLI equivalent of the TUI Analytics screen.

## Anomaly Detection Categories

### 1. ack_sla — Acknowledgment SLA violations
- **Trigger**: Messages with ack_required=true, ack_ts IS NULL, created_at < now - 1800s (30min)
- **Severity**: warn (30min-2h), error (>2h)
- **Remediation**: `am mail ack --project P --agent A <message_ids>`

### 2. reservation_ttl — Expiring reservations
- **Trigger**: Reservations with remaining < 5 minutes
- **Severity**: info (<10min), warn (<5min), error (<1min)
- **Remediation**: `am file_reservations renew P A --paths <path> --extend 3600`

### 3. tool_reliability — Tool error rates
- **Trigger**: Tool error_pct > 10%
- **Severity**: warn (10-25%), error (>25%)
- **Remediation**: `am robot metrics` (investigate)

### 4. communication_gap — Silent agents
- **Trigger**: Agent last_active > 2 hours AND has active reservations
- **Severity**: info (2-6h), warn (>6h)
- **Remediation**: `am robot agents --active` (check who's working)

### 5. inbox_overflow — Large unread count
- **Trigger**: Unread messages > 20 for any agent
- **Severity**: warn (>20), error (>50)
- **Remediation**: `am robot inbox --limit 50`

## Output Format
```
anomalies[2]:
  - severity: warn
    confidence: 0.85
    category: ack_sla
    headline: 2 messages pending ack > 30 minutes
    rationale: Messages 142, 156 have ack_required=true but no ack_ts
    remediation: am mail ack --project P --agent A 142 156
    related_screen: inbox

  - severity: info
    confidence: 0.72
    category: reservation_ttl
    headline: 1 reservation expiring in < 5 minutes
    rationale: docs/API.md exclusive lock expires in 3 minutes
    remediation: am file_reservations renew P A --paths docs/API.md --extend 3600
    related_screen: reservations

summary:
  total_anomalies: 2
  by_severity: warn(1),info(1)
  by_category: ack_sla(1),reservation_ttl(1)
```

## Confidence Scoring
- ack_sla: 0.85 base (clear SLA violation)
- reservation_ttl: 0.72 base (agent might intentionally let it expire)
- tool_reliability: 0.60 base (could be transient)
- communication_gap: 0.50 base (agent might be in deep work)
- inbox_overflow: 0.65 base (might be intentional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 5 anomaly categories implemented
- Severity escalation works (warn→error based on thresholds)
- Remediation commands are valid, copy-paste ready
- Confidence scores reflect detection certainty
- Empty anomalies produce clean output
- related_screen field helps agents navigate to relevant data

 {"id":"br-58bpv","title":"R4.5: Unit tests for reservations, metrics, health, analytics commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:19.582253696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:24:27.868733972Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:24:27.868715417Z","close_reason":"10 unit tests: MetricEntry/HealthProbe/AnomalyCard serialization, TOON round-trips, envelope formatting for metrics/health/analytics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-58bpv","depends_on_id":"br-2ui21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:54.813774263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-58bpv","depends_on_id":"br-3o7hh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:55.273143021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-58bpv","depends_on_id":"br-3p7th","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:55.041017669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-58bpv","depends_on_id":"br-56kxi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:55.508686691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":317,"issue_id":"br-58bpv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R4.5: Track 4 Unit Tests

## What
Unit tests for all 4 monitoring & analytics commands.

## Test Cases (minimum 18 tests)

### robot reservations (5 tests)
1. `test_reservations_my_filtered` — only current agent's reservations in my_reservations
2. `test_reservations_all_flag` — --all shows all agents' reservations
3. `test_reservations_expiring_warning` — ⚠ marker on <10min remaining
4. `test_reservations_conflict_detection` — overlapping exclusive patterns detected
5. `test_reservations_actions_renew` — _actions suggest renew for expiring items

### robot metrics (4 tests)
6. `test_metrics_summary_totals` — total_calls and total_errors correct
7. `test_metrics_error_rate_alert` — >10% error rate triggers warn alert
8. `test_metrics_slow_tool_alert` — >2000ms avg triggers warn alert
9. `test_metrics_empty` — no metrics data → clean output with zeros

### robot health (5 tests)
10. `test_health_db_probe` — DB probe succeeds with valid connection
11. `test_health_disk_sizes` — file sizes reported correctly
12. `test_health_server_not_running` — TCP probe fails gracefully
13. `test_health_level_healthy` — all probes ok → "healthy"
14. `test_health_level_degraded` — some probes failed → "degraded"

### robot analytics (4 tests)
15. `test_analytics_ack_sla_detection` — overdue ack messages detected
16. `test_analytics_reservation_ttl_detection` — expiring reservations detected
17. `test_analytics_severity_escalation` — 30min→warn, 2h→error for ack_sla
18. `test_analytics_remediation_commands` — generated commands are valid

## Test Setup
- Seed DB with known data:
  - Messages with specific ack_ts states and timestamps
  - Reservations with various TTLs
  - Tool metrics with known error rates
- Use `open_db_sync()` pattern
- For health tests: skip TCP probe (can't guarantee server running in test)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 18+ tests pass
- Tests use real DB queries
- Analytics tests verify correct anomaly detection
- Edge cases covered (empty data, all healthy, all broken)
